sglang.title: [AMD][HIP] NSA: bf16 passthrough from RMSNorm to eliminate FP8 dequantization
sglang.author: Jacob0226
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When running GLM-5-FP8 on MI355X with FP8 quantization, the NSA indexer's head gate path (weights_proj) requires bf16 input. However, the upstream fused_rms_fp8_group_quant only outputs FP8 tensors, discarding the bf16 intermediate from RMSNorm. This forces a redundant FP8-to-bf16 dequant

sglang.title: Add page_size to admission token budget check
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Add page_size to total_tokens in PrefillAdder.add_one_req to match the budget deduction in _update_prefill_budget, which already accounts for page-alignment overhead.
Without this, the admission check is more optimistic than the actual budget deduction. When the pool is nearly full, a reques

This PR fixes #22311
Qwen3.5-27B takes a fallback BA path (after commit 5bdc07d) that returns non-contiguous split views for a and b. The existing GDN Triton kernels assumed contiguous layouts and hardcoded the batch or token stride, which caused incorrect memory reads and the observed ac

The main goal is to make ModelOpt FP8 practical for SGLang diffusion models without requiring users to manually reconstruct F

sglang.title: 【BugFix】fix the bug of minimax_m2.5 model that causes repeated outputs when using tp16
sglang.author: kingkingleeljj
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
MiniMax M2.5 uses 8 KV heads. When running with TP=16, the tensor parallel size exceeds the number of KV heads, meaning multiple TP ranks must share (replicate) the same KV head. The existing MiniMaxM2RMSNormTP computes its weight shard size as hidden_size / attn_tp_size, which for the K

sglang.title: perf: enable inductor combo_kernels for horizontal fusion
sglang.author: jasperjiaguo
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Enable combo_kernels and benchmark_combo_kernel in inductor config to allow horizontal fusion of sibling ops with different shapes. This fuses operations like q_norm + k_norm (QK normalization) into a single triton kernel instead of generating separate kernels for each.
Requires torch >= 2.9.0.


Pr

sglang.title: fix: server crash when stop_token_ids contains null
sglang.author: KrishnanPrash
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #22144
stop_token_ids: [null] in a JSON request propagates None through to torch.tensor() in min_new_tokens.py,
crashing the scheduler. Filter None values in SamplingParams.__init__.

sglang.title: Reduce GPU memory for MoE parallel groups
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When expert parallelism (EP) is enabled, initialize_model_parallel creates MOE_EP and MOE_TP groups with full communicator stacks (pynccl + custom_allreduce), each costing ~700 MB of GPU memory. For example, with --tp 8 --ep 4, torch distributed init uses ~2.7 GB vs ~1.3 GB for pure --tp

Call get_scheduler_metadata once per batch in decode metadata init (including CUDA graph capture/replay paths) and pass the result to flash_attn_with_kvcache so FA3 skips the internal prepare_varlen_num_blocks kernel on every layer.
This eliminates 63 redundant GPU kernel calls per decode st

sglang.title: feat: add weekly workflow to update CI test est_time values
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The est_time values in CI test registration calls (register_cuda_ci, register_cpu_ci) drift from actual execution times over time. These values drive the LPT load-balancing algorithm that partitions tests across parallel CI jobs. Inaccurate estimates lead to unbalanced partitions, wasting

Adds a nightly CI pipeline that runs DeepSeek-R1 inference benchmarks on the GB200 cluster using dynamo-sglang via srt-slurm. Benchmarks run daily at 2 AM UTC on a self-hosted gb200 runner backed by a Slurm cluster.

PR #22545 introduced the weekly update_est_time.py script that updates est_time values from CI data. However, it keyed timings by (filename, backend) instead of (filename, suite). This caused files with multiple register_cuda_ci calls for different suites (e.g., H100 vs B200) to get the s

#22365 added _resolve_quant_config_from_transformer_override which calls maybe_download_model on t

sglang.title: fix: match est_time updates by backend, not just suite
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The update_est_time.py script uses register_\w+_ci in its regex, which matches any backend. When a test file has both register_cuda_ci and register_amd_ci with the same suite (e.g., test_eagle3_basic.py with suite="stage-b-test-1-gpu-small"), the CUDA median gets incorrectly applied to th

sglang.title: chore: update CI test est_time values
sglang.author: sglang-bot
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Updates est_time values in CI test registration calls based on the median of the last 10 successful executions from scheduled PR Test runs on main.
This keeps the LPT load-balancing algorithm accurate for partitioning tests across parallel CI jobs.
🤖 Generated with GitHub Actions

vllm.title: [Perf] Reduce H2D pageable memory copies
vllm.author: jackcfwang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix H2D (Host-to-Device) pageable memory copy bottleneck in  attention Triton kernel, which caused pipeline bubbles between transformer blocks.
Root Cause: mm_prefix_range_tensor() recomputes and transfers data from CPU to GPU on every attention call, generating redundant H2D pageable copies

vllm.title: [Kernel][Hardware][AMD] Add TritonW4A16LinearKernel for ROCm
vllm.author: jatseng-ai
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Add Triton-based W4A16 GEMM kernel for INT4 weight / FP16 activation inference on AMD MI300 (ROCm)
Supports symmetric (uint4b8) and asymmetric (uint4) quantization with grouped scales
Registers as preferred mixed-precision linear kernel on ROCm platform

vllm.title: [Performance] Remove unnecessary zero-fill of MLA decode output tensor in Aiter backend
vllm.author: xaguilar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
During the MLA decode phase on ROCm with the Aiter backend, a
vectorized_elementwise GPU kernel is launched to zero-fill the output tensor
before the MLA kernel overwrites it entirely. This adds unnecessary kernel
launch latency on the critical decode path.
Root
In rocm_aiter_mla.py, the out

vllm.title: feat: add logit_scale to PoolerConfig for affine score calibration
vllm.author: jefp
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add logit_scale to PoolerConfig alongside existing logit_bias, enabling affine score calibration (Platt scaling) in the pooler: activation(scale * (logit - bias)).
This allows reranker and classification models to produce calibrated probability scores via --pooler-config without custom model

vllm.title: perf(moe): add tuned fused_moe config for RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
vllm.author: efortin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add tuned fused_moe Triton kernel configs for NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (SM120) with FP8 block-quantized MoE.
Three configs added:

E=256, N=512 (TP=1) -- covers Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-FP8 and similar 256-expert models at TP=1
E=256, N=384 (TP=4, tensor parallel) -- covers 256-ex

9 pytest edge-case tests for MultiConnector (NixlConnector + OffloadingConnector) in a PD setup

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix FlashInfer crash with kv_cache_dtype_skip_layers
vllm.author: yzong-rh
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix FlashInfer attention crashing when kv_cache_dtype_skip_layers is used (introduced in #33695).
The metadata builder read the global cache_config.cache_dtype (e.g., "fp8") instead of the per-group kv_cache_spec.kv_quant_mode, so skipped layers were still treated as fp8.
Test Plan
On B200s

vllm.title: Fix the order of _free_encoder_inputs
vllm.author: gty111
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
#38551

freeable can be triggered before fetch due to an inconsistency in how num_computed_tokens is updated.
After a request is scheduled, the scheduler immediately updates the token counter:
request.num_computed_tokens += num_scheduled_tokens
However, at this point the GPU has not actually

vllm.title: [Bugfix] add SupportsMultiModal to Exaone4_5_MTP
vllm.author: elwhyjay
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Exaone4_5_MTP is missing the SupportsMultiModal interface, causing MTP speculative decoding to break for the Exaone4.5 VL model.
In eagle.py:1310-1318, the spec decode code calls self.model.embed_input_ids(...) on the draft model. Since Exaone4_5_MTP lacks this method, it raises AttributeErr

vllm.title: [Misc] Update deprecation warning for --model flag
vllm.author: z1ying
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #39506
Problem
The deprecation warning for the --model flag in vllm serve currently references removal in v0.13.
Since vLLM is now at v0.19, this warning is outdated and misleading.
Solution
Update the warning message to refer to "a future version" instead of a specific outdated versio
